title: ajuga ophrydis
intro: Ajuga ophrydis, also known as bugleweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the mint family and is commonly found in Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to inhibit the conversion of thyroxine to triiodothyronine, making it useful in treating hyperthyroidism. The health benefits of Ajuga ophrydis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the severity of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, bugleweed is often used as a tincture, tea, or dried extract, and can be combined with other herbs to create a range of formulas for various health conditions.
